info.title: Timesheet API
info.description:
Complete REST API for the Timesheet workforce management system. Handles employee time tracking, scheduling, roster management, payroll and analytics.

## Authentication
Uses cookie-based JWT authentication. Three separate auth layers:
- Admin/user: login via /api/auth/login
- Employee: login via /api/employee/login
- Device: register via /api/device/register

## Roles
- super_admin: full system access
- admin: manage employees and schedules
- user: limited view-only dashboard access
- employee: clock in/out and own timesheets

## Base URL
All endpoints are prefixed with /api

## Error Responses
All errors follow the format: { error: string, message?: string }

## Cookie Auth
All protected endpoints require valid JWT cookie sent automatically via credentials: include on all requests.

username.description: Unique username for admin or user account. Case-insensitive, must match existing user record.

password.description: User password. Must be at least 8 characters long and match the bcrypt-hashed password in database.

pin.description: Employee 4-digit PIN. Must match an active employee record and be exactly 4 numeric digits.

id.description: Unique identifier for the authenticated user account.

username.description: Username of the authenticated user account.

role.description: Role level determining system access permissions. super_admin has full access, admin can manage employees, user has limited access.

location.description: Primary location assignment for the user, used for access control and filtering.

token.description: JWT authentication token for subsequent API requests. Set as httpOnly cookie automatically.

description.example: Responsible for customer service and sales

post.summary: Admin/user login with username and password
post.description: Authenticates admin or user accounts using username and password credentials. Creates a secure JWT session cookie for subsequent API requests. Supports super_admin, admin, and user roles with different permission levels. Returns user profile and role information upon successful authentication.

username.description: Unique username for admin or user account. Case-insensitive, must match existing user record.

password.description: User password. Must be at least 8 characters long and match the bcrypt-hashed password in database.

post.summary: Admin/user logout and session termination
post.description: Terminates the current admin or user session by invalidating the JWT cookie. Clears authentication state and requires re-login for subsequent protected requests. Safe to call multiple times - returns success even if no active session exists.

get.summary: Get current authenticated user profile
get.description: Returns the profile information for the currently authenticated admin or user. Includes role, permissions, assigned locations, and account details. Requires valid JWT session cookie. Used to verify authentication status and load user context in frontend applications.

post.summary: Change current user password
post.description: Updates the password for the currently authenticated admin or user. Requires the current password for verification before setting the new password. The new password must meet minimum security requirements (8+ characters). Automatically invalidates other active sessions for security. Accessible by authenticated admin and user roles only.

post.summary: Request password reset email
post.description: Initiates a password reset process by sending a secure reset link to the user's email address. Generates a time-limited reset token that expires after 1 hour. The email contains a link to the reset password page with the token. Safe to call with non-existent emails (returns success to prevent email enumeration). Accessible without authentication.

email.description: Email address of the user requesting password reset. Must be a valid email format. System will send reset instructions if account exists.

post.summary: Reset password using email token
post.description: Completes the password reset process using a valid reset token received via email. The token must be valid and not expired (1-hour limit). Sets a new password for the user account and invalidates the reset token. Automatically logs out all existing sessions for security. Accessible without authentication but requires valid token.

token.description: Password reset token received via email. Must be valid and not expired (1-hour limit from generation).

newPassword.description: New password to set for the user account. Must be at least 8 characters long and will be bcrypt-hashed before storage.

post.summary: Setup password for new user account
post.description: Allows new users to set their initial password using a setup token sent via email during account creation. The setup token is single-use and expires after 24 hours. Once password is set, the user can login normally with username and password. Used for employee accounts created by admins with 'sendSetupEmail' option. Accessible without authentication but requires valid setup token.

token.description: Setup token received via email when account was created. Single-use token that expires after 24 hours.

newPassword.description: Initial password for the new user account. Must be at least 8 characters long and will be bcrypt-hashed before storage.

post.summary: Unified login endpoint for both admin and employee authentication
post.description: Flexible authentication endpoint that accepts either username/password for admin/user accounts or PIN for employee accounts. Automatically detects the authentication type based on the request payload. Creates appropriate JWT session cookies and returns user profile information. Used by frontend applications to provide a single login interface for all user types.

get.summary: List all users with pagination and search
get.description: Returns a paginated list of all admin and user accounts in the system. Supports search filtering by username and pagination controls. Includes user roles, permissions, assigned locations, and account status. Only accessible by admin and super_admin roles. User role can only see limited information about other users.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search term to filter users by username. Case-insensitive partial matching.

post.summary: Create new admin or user account
post.description: Creates a new admin or user account with specified role and permissions. Validates username uniqueness and password strength requirements. Assigns location access and role-based permissions. Only accessible by super_admin and admin roles. Automatically hashes passwords using bcrypt before storage. Optionally sends setup email for initial password configuration.

get.summary: Get user details by ID
get.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific user account by ID. Returns user profile, role, permissions, assigned locations, and account metadata. Access is restricted based on user role - super_admin can view all users, admin can view users in their managed locations, user role has limited access to other user profiles.

patch.description: Updates user account details including username, role, permissions, and location assignments. Validates role changes and permission modifications based on current user's authority level. Only super_admin can modify other super_admin accounts. Admin users can modify user accounts within their managed locations. Automatically logs all changes for audit purposes.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a user account from the system. Validates that the user is not currently logged in and has no pending operations. Only accessible by super_admin role. Cannot delete the last super_admin account to prevent system lockout. All associated data and permissions are removed. This action cannot be undone.

get.summary: List all employees with filtering and pagination
get.description: Returns a paginated list of all employees with optional filtering by location, role, employer, and search terms. Supports sorting by various fields. Includes employee profile information, assigned roles, locations, and current status. Accessible by admin and user roles with appropriate permissions.

post.summary: Create new employee record
post.description: Creates a new employee with profile information, PIN assignment, role and location assignments. Automatically generates secure PIN if not provided. Optionally sends setup email for password creation. Validates PIN uniqueness and required fields. Creates initial employee record for time tracking and scheduling.

get.summary: Get employee details by ID
get.description: Retrieves comprehensive employee information including profile data, assigned roles, locations, employment details, and current status. Returns timesheet summary, award information, and availability data. Access is filtered based on user permissions and location assignments. Used by admin interfaces for employee management and profile viewing.

patch.summary: Update employee profile and employment details
patch.description: Updates employee information including personal details, employment status, role assignments, and location access. Validates PIN uniqueness if changed. Updates profile photo via Cloudinary integration. Manages role and location assignments with proper validation. Accessible by admin and super_admin roles with appropriate location permissions.

delete.description: Permanently removes an employee from the system including all associated timesheet data, role assignments, and profile information. Validates that the employee is not currently clocked in. Archives timesheet data for payroll compliance before deletion. Only accessible by admin and super_admin roles. This action cannot be undone.

get.summary: Get employee timesheet entries with date filtering
get.description: Retrieves timesheet entries for a specific employee within the specified date range. Returns clock-in/out times, break durations, total hours worked, and any associated notes or flags. Supports pagination and date filtering. Accessible by admin users and the employee themselves. Used for payroll processing and attendance tracking.

patch.description: Allows admin users to modify timesheet entries for corrections or adjustments. Can update clock-out times, break durations, and add administrative notes. Automatically recalculates total hours worked. Logs all changes for audit purposes. Requires admin or super_admin role. Used for correcting time tracking errors or manual adjustments.

get.summary: Get employee weekly availability schedule
get.description: Retrieves the employee's availability schedule showing which days and hours they are available to work. Returns day-of-week patterns with start/end times and availability status. Used by roster management system for shift scheduling and planning. Accessible by admin users and scheduling managers for workforce planning.

post.summary: Set employee availability for specific day and time
post.description: Creates or updates employee availability for a specific day of the week with start and end times. Validates time format and prevents scheduling conflicts. Used by managers to set when employees are available for shifts. Supports recurring weekly patterns and temporary availability changes. Accessible by admin users and the employee themselves.

delete.description: Deletes employee availability settings, making them unavailable for scheduling during the specified periods. Used when employees are no longer available for certain shifts or days. Validates that no existing shifts are scheduled during the deleted availability periods. Accessible by admin users and scheduling managers.

get.summary: Get employee absence records and leave requests
get.description: Retrieves all absence records for an employee including sick leave, vacation time, personal days, and other approved absences. Returns absence dates, types, approval status, and reasons. Used for leave management and attendance tracking. Accessible by admin users, HR personnel, and the employee themselves for viewing their absence history.

post.summary: Create new absence record or leave request
post.description: Creates a new absence record for an employee specifying the dates, type of absence, and reason. Supports various absence types including sick leave, vacation, personal time, and other categories. Automatically calculates duration and validates against available leave balances. Requires approval workflow for certain absence types. Accessible by admin users and employees for self-service leave requests.

get.summary: Get employee workplace conditions and accommodations
get.description: Retrieves special workplace conditions, medical accommodations, and accessibility requirements for an employee. Returns condition types, descriptions, and required accommodations. Used for compliance with disability laws and workplace safety requirements. Access is restricted to HR personnel and managers with appropriate permissions due to sensitive medical information.

get.summary: Get employee award and pay rate history
get.description: Retrieves the complete history of award assignments and pay rate changes for an employee. Returns award names, levels, effective dates, and hourly rates over time. Used for payroll processing, compliance reporting, and tracking employee progression. Essential for calculating correct pay rates and overtime calculations. Accessible by admin users and payroll personnel.

post.summary: Assign new award or update pay rate for employee
post.description: Assigns a new award classification or updates the pay rate level for an employee. Validates award exists and level is valid. Creates historical record of the change with effective date. Automatically updates payroll calculations for future timesheets. Used for promotions, pay increases, and award reclassifications. Accessible by admin users and HR personnel with payroll permissions.

get.summary: Get availability summary for all employees
get.description: Retrieves availability information for all employees, optionally filtered by date and location. Returns employee names, availability status, and available hours for workforce planning. Used by scheduling managers to identify available staff for shift assignments. Supports filtering by specific dates and locations for targeted scheduling needs.

get.summary: Check if employee PIN already exists in system
get.description: Validates whether a specific PIN is already assigned to an employee to prevent duplicates. Returns existence status and associated employee ID if found. Used during employee creation and PIN updates to ensure uniqueness. Essential for maintaining security and preventing PIN conflicts in the time clock system. Accessible by admin users during employee management.

get.summary: Generate unique PIN for new employee
get.description: Automatically generates a unique 4-digit PIN that is not currently assigned to any employee. Uses secure random number generation and validates uniqueness against existing employee PINs. Used during employee creation process to assign secure time clock access codes. Ensures PIN uniqueness and follows security best practices for employee authentication.

get.summary: Get employee photo synchronization status
get.description: Returns statistics about employee profile photo synchronization including total employees, those with photos, those without photos, and last sync date. Used for monitoring photo management and identifying employees missing profile pictures. Helps maintain complete employee records and supports face recognition features in time clock systems.

post.summary: Synchronize employee photos from external source
post.description: Initiates bulk synchronization of employee profile photos from external systems or cloud storage. Processes multiple employee photos, handles image optimization, and updates profile records. Returns sync results including successful uploads and failed attempts. Used for bulk photo management and maintaining up-to-date employee profiles for identification purposes.

post.summary: Employee PIN-based login for time clock
post.description: Authenticates employees using their 4-6 digit PIN for time clock access. Creates a secure JWT session cookie for subsequent clock operations. Requires a valid registered device token. Returns employee profile information and current clock status. Used exclusively on registered kiosk devices for employee time tracking.

post.summary: Employee logout from time clock system
post.description: Terminates the current employee session and invalidates the JWT token. Clears authentication state and requires re-login for subsequent time clock operations. Safe to call multiple times and handles already-logged-out states gracefully. Used when employees finish their shift or when switching users on shared kiosk devices.

get.summary: Get current authenticated employee profile
get.description: Returns the profile information for the currently authenticated employee including name, PIN, assigned roles, locations, and current clock status. Used by time clock interfaces to display employee information and determine available actions. Requires valid employee JWT session token from PIN-based login.

get.summary: Get employee's own timesheet entries
get.description: Retrieves timesheet entries for the currently authenticated employee within the specified date range. Employees can only view their own timesheet data for privacy and security. Returns clock-in/out times, break durations, total hours, and any notes. Used by employee self-service interfaces for viewing work history and hours worked.

post.summary: Change employee's own password
post.description: Allows employees to change their own password for account security. Requires current password verification before setting new password. New password must meet minimum security requirements. Automatically invalidates other active sessions for security. Used by employee self-service interfaces for password management and security maintenance.

post.summary: Create offline session for disconnected operation
post.description: Creates an offline session token that allows time clock operations when network connectivity is unavailable. Stores session data locally and synchronizes when connection is restored. Used by kiosk devices in areas with unreliable internet connectivity. Ensures employees can still clock in/out during network outages without losing time tracking data.

post.summary: Upload employee clock-in photo or profile image
post.description: Uploads employee photos captured during clock-in operations or for profile updates. Supports image optimization, format validation, and secure storage via Cloudinary integration. Used for identity verification during time clock operations and maintaining current employee profile photos. Handles image compression and generates secure URLs for storage.

400.description: Invalid file or file too large

post.summary: Clock employee in, out, or manage breaks
post.description: Records a clock-in, clock-out, break start, or break end event for an authenticated employee. Automatically calculates total hours worked when clocking out. Captures optional photo via webcam and GPS location for verification. Validates geofencing rules if configured. Accessible by authenticated employees on registered devices only. Creates timesheet entries with automatic flagging for missing data.

action.description: Type of clock action to perform. 'in' starts work shift, 'out' ends shift, 'break' starts break period, 'endBreak' resumes work from break.

timestamp.description: Optional custom timestamp for the clock action. If not provided, uses current server time. Must be in ISO 8601 format.

locationId.description: Optional location identifier where the clock action occurred. Used for location-based validation and reporting.

notes.description: Optional notes or comments about the clock action. Limited to 500 characters. Useful for explaining unusual circumstances or shift changes.

photo.description: Optional base64-encoded image data captured during clock action. Used for identity verification and security. Automatically uploaded to Cloudinary if provided.

get.summary: Get all rosters with filtering options
get.description: Retrieves all roster schedules with optional filtering by date range and location. Returns roster summaries including week identifiers, publication status, total shifts, and total hours. Used by scheduling managers to view and manage workforce schedules across multiple locations and time periods. Supports pagination for large datasets.

get.summary: Get detailed roster for specific week
get.description: Retrieves complete roster details for a specific week including all scheduled shifts, employee assignments, locations, and roles. Returns shift times, break durations, and any special notes. Used by managers and employees to view weekly schedules and plan work assignments. Essential for workforce coordination and schedule communication.

post.summary: Generate automated roster for specified week
post.description: Automatically generates a roster for the specified week using employee availability, role requirements, and business rules. Can use predefined templates or create from scratch based on location needs. Considers employee preferences, labor costs, and compliance requirements. Used by scheduling managers to quickly create optimized work schedules while ensuring adequate coverage.

post.summary: Publish roster to make it visible to employees
post.description: Publishes a roster making it visible to employees and finalizing the schedule. Once published, employees can view their shifts and the roster becomes the official work schedule. Validates roster completeness and compliance before publication. Triggers notifications to employees about their scheduled shifts. Cannot be easily modified after publication.

post.summary: Unpublish roster to allow modifications
post.description: Unpublishes a roster making it invisible to employees and allowing modifications. Used when schedules need to be changed after publication or when errors are discovered. Employees will no longer see the roster until it is republished. Notifies affected employees about the schedule change. Should be used sparingly to maintain schedule reliability.

get.summary: Get all shifts for specific roster week
get.description: Retrieves all individual shifts within a specific roster week including employee assignments, locations, roles, and timing details. Returns comprehensive shift information for detailed schedule management. Used by managers to review and modify individual shifts within the weekly roster. Essential for fine-tuning schedules and managing shift assignments.

post.summary: Add new shift to existing roster
post.description: Creates a new shift within an existing roster week with specified employee, location, role, and timing. Validates employee availability and prevents scheduling conflicts. Used for adding extra shifts, covering absences, or adjusting schedules after initial roster generation. Automatically checks compliance with labor laws and employee preferences.

patch.summary: Update existing shift details in roster
patch.description: Modifies an existing shift within a roster including timing, employee assignment, location, or role changes. Validates changes against employee availability and business rules. Used for schedule adjustments, employee substitutions, or correcting scheduling errors. Automatically recalculates roster totals and checks compliance requirements.

delete.description: Deletes a specific shift from the roster schedule. Validates that the shift can be safely removed without violating minimum staffing requirements. Used for canceling shifts due to low demand, employee unavailability, or schedule optimization. Automatically updates roster totals and notifies affected employees of the cancellation.

get.summary: Identify understaffed periods in roster
get.description: Analyzes the roster to identify time periods and locations where staffing levels are below requirements. Returns gaps showing required vs scheduled staff, duration of understaffing, and affected roles. Used by managers to identify scheduling problems and ensure adequate coverage. Critical for maintaining service levels and operational efficiency.

post.summary: Automatically fill understaffed periods
post.description: Automatically assigns available employees to fill identified gaps in the roster schedule. Uses employee availability, preferences, and business rules to optimize assignments. Considers labor costs, skill requirements, and compliance constraints. Used to quickly resolve staffing shortages while maintaining schedule quality and employee satisfaction.

get.summary: Validate roster compliance with labor laws
get.description: Analyzes the roster for compliance with labor laws, award conditions, and company policies. Identifies violations such as excessive overtime, insufficient breaks, or minimum rest period breaches. Returns detailed compliance report with violation severity levels. Essential for legal compliance and avoiding labor law penalties.

description.example: Employee scheduled for 45 hours, exceeds 40 hour limit
Shift longer than 6 hours without adequate break

get.summary: Get all categories with search and pagination
get.description: Retrieves all system categories including roles, locations, employers, and other classification types. Supports search filtering and pagination for large datasets. Categories are used throughout the system for organizing employees, locations, and business entities. Essential for maintaining consistent data classification and reporting structures.

post.summary: Create new category for system classification
post.description: Creates a new category for classifying employees, locations, roles, or other business entities. Validates category name uniqueness and assigns color coding for visual identification. Used for organizing system data and enabling filtered reporting. Categories support the organizational structure and data management throughout the application.

description.example: Responsible for customer service and sales

get.summary: Get comprehensive dashboard statistics
get.description: Retrieves key performance indicators and statistics for the dashboard including employee counts, hours worked, attendance rates, and operational metrics. Supports filtering by location and date range for targeted reporting. Used by managers and administrators to monitor workforce performance and make data-driven decisions.

get.summary: Get all available schedule templates
get.description: Retrieves all predefined schedule templates that can be used for roster generation. Templates contain standard shift patterns, role assignments, and timing configurations. Used by scheduling managers to quickly create consistent rosters based on proven patterns. Supports different business models and operational requirements.

post.summary: Create new reusable schedule template
post.description: Creates a new schedule template with predefined shift patterns that can be reused for roster generation. Templates include day-of-week patterns, shift times, role requirements, and location assignments. Used to standardize scheduling practices and speed up roster creation. Essential for maintaining consistent scheduling across multiple locations.

post.summary: Copy schedule from existing template
post.description: Creates a new roster by copying shifts and patterns from an existing template. Applies template to specified week and locations with automatic employee assignment based on availability and roles. Used to quickly generate rosters using proven scheduling patterns. Saves time and ensures consistency in workforce scheduling.

get.summary: Get role availability and staffing levels
get.description: Retrieves availability information for different roles including available employees, scheduled employees, and required staffing levels. Supports filtering by location and date for targeted workforce planning. Used by scheduling managers to ensure adequate role coverage and identify staffing gaps or surpluses.

get.summary: Get all shift swap requests with filtering
get.description: Retrieves all shift swap requests with optional filtering by status and employee. Returns request details including requester, target employee, shift information, and approval status. Used by managers to review and process shift swap requests. Employees can view their own swap requests and responses from other employees.

post.summary: Create new shift swap request
post.description: Creates a new request to swap shifts with another employee. Validates that both employees are qualified for each other's roles and shifts don't conflict with availability. Initiates approval workflow and notifies target employee. Used by employees to request schedule changes and manage work-life balance while maintaining operational coverage.

get.summary: Get detailed shift swap request information
get.description: Retrieves comprehensive details for a specific shift swap request including all parties involved, shift details, request reason, current status, and any administrative notes. Used by managers for reviewing swap requests and by employees to track their request status and responses.

patch.summary: Update shift swap request status or details
patch.description: Updates shift swap request information including status changes and administrative notes. Used by managers to approve, deny, or modify swap requests. Can add notes explaining decisions or requirements. Automatically notifies involved employees of status changes and updates roster if approved.

delete.summary: Cancel and remove shift swap request
delete.description: Permanently deletes a shift swap request from the system. Can only be done by the original requester or administrators. Used when employees no longer need the swap or when cleaning up old requests. Notifies all involved parties of the cancellation and removes the request from pending queues.

post.summary: Accept incoming shift swap request
post.description: Allows the target employee to accept a shift swap request directed at them. Validates that the employee is still available and qualified for the requested shift. Moves the request to pending manager approval if required. Used by employees to respond positively to swap requests from colleagues.

post.summary: Manager approval of shift swap request
post.description: Allows managers to officially approve a shift swap request that has been accepted by both employees. Validates business rules, staffing requirements, and compliance before final approval. Updates the roster with the new shift assignments and notifies all parties. Final step in the shift swap approval process.

post.summary: Deny shift swap request with reason
post.description: Allows managers to deny a shift swap request with a required explanation. Used when swaps would violate business rules, create staffing problems, or conflict with operational requirements. Notifies all involved employees of the denial and reason. Helps maintain schedule integrity while providing transparency in decision-making.

get.summary: Get detailed hours summary and breakdown
get.description: Retrieves comprehensive hours summary including total hours, regular hours, overtime hours, and break time breakdowns. Supports filtering by date range and location for targeted analysis. Used for payroll preparation, labor cost analysis, and operational reporting. Essential for understanding workforce utilization and costs.

get.summary: Get all award classifications and pay rates
get.description: Retrieves all available award classifications including pay levels, hourly rates, and descriptions. Awards define pay structures and employment conditions for different employee categories. Used for employee classification, payroll processing, and compliance with industrial relations requirements. Essential for maintaining proper pay rates and conditions.

post.summary: Create new award classification with pay levels
post.description: Creates a new award classification defining pay rates and employment conditions for employee categories. Includes multiple pay levels with corresponding hourly rates. Used for establishing pay structures that comply with industrial relations requirements and company policies. Essential for proper employee classification and payroll management.

get.summary: Get all timesheet entries with comprehensive filtering
get.description: Retrieves timesheet entries with extensive filtering options including employee, date range, and location. Returns clock-in/out times, break durations, total hours, and any flags or notes. Used for payroll processing, attendance monitoring, and operational reporting. Supports pagination for large datasets and export functionality.

post.summary: Create manual timesheet entry for employee
post.description: Creates a new timesheet entry manually for situations where automatic time tracking was not possible. Validates employee exists and prevents duplicate entries for the same time period. Used for correcting missed clock-ins, adding manual entries for remote work, or handling system outages. Requires administrative privileges and audit logging.

get.summary: Generate comprehensive employee performance report
get.description: Generates detailed analytics report for a specific employee including hours worked, punctuality metrics, attendance rates, and performance indicators. Supports date range filtering for targeted analysis. Used by managers for performance reviews, identifying training needs, and recognizing high performers. Essential for data-driven employee management.

get.summary: Get system activity logs for audit purposes
get.description: Retrieves comprehensive activity logs showing user actions, system changes, and administrative operations. Supports filtering by user, action type, resource, and date range. Used for security auditing, compliance reporting, and troubleshooting system issues. Essential for maintaining system security and accountability.

get.summary: Get current email server configuration
get.description: Retrieves current email server settings including SMTP configuration, sender information, and connection parameters. Used by administrators to review and troubleshoot email functionality. Sensitive credentials are masked for security. Essential for maintaining email notifications and system communications.

post.summary: Update email server configuration settings
post.description: Updates email server configuration including SMTP settings, authentication credentials, and sender information. Validates connection parameters and tests email functionality. Used by administrators to configure email notifications, password reset functionality, and system communications. Requires super_admin privileges for security.

get.summary: Get file storage and upload configuration
get.description: Retrieves current file storage settings including maximum file sizes, allowed file types, compression settings, and retention policies. Used by administrators to manage storage resources and configure upload restrictions. Essential for maintaining system performance and controlling storage costs.

get.summary: Check system setup and initialization status
get.description: Returns the current setup status of the system including whether initial admin account exists and system is properly configured. Used during initial system deployment and for troubleshooting setup issues. Accessible without authentication to support initial system configuration process.

post.summary: Create initial system administrator account
post.description: Creates the first administrator account during system setup process. Can only be used once when no admin accounts exist. Validates username uniqueness and password strength. Automatically assigns super_admin role with full system access. Essential for initial system configuration and security setup.

400.description: Invalid request data or setup already completed

get.summary: Get system feature flags and configuration
get.description: Retrieves current feature flag settings that control system functionality and experimental features. Used by frontend applications to enable/disable features dynamically. Includes flags for face recognition, offline mode, analytics, and maintenance mode. Accessible without authentication for public feature availability.

get.summary: Get calendar events and scheduled activities
get.description: Retrieves calendar events including meetings, training sessions, company events, and other scheduled activities. Supports filtering by date range and location for targeted event viewing. Used by managers and employees to view upcoming events and plan schedules around company activities.

post.summary: Upload image files to cloud storage
post.description: Uploads image files to secure cloud storage with automatic optimization and format validation. Supports employee profile photos, clock-in verification images, and other business-related images. Handles image compression, format conversion, and generates secure URLs for access. Integrates with Cloudinary for reliable image management.

400.description: Invalid file or file too large

post.summary: Link timesheet entry to scheduled shift
post.description: Associates a timesheet entry with a specific scheduled shift for accurate tracking and reporting. Validates that the timesheet and shift belong to the same employee and time period. Used for connecting actual worked hours with planned schedules. Essential for roster accuracy and payroll reconciliation.

get.summary: Get timesheet entry associated with specific shift
get.description: Retrieves the timesheet entry that corresponds to a specific scheduled shift. Used for comparing planned vs actual hours worked and identifying discrepancies. Essential for roster management and ensuring employees work their assigned shifts. Helps managers track shift compliance and attendance.

get.summary: Get payroll data with hours and pay calculations
get.description: Retrieves comprehensive payroll data including total hours, regular hours, overtime hours, and calculated gross pay for employees. Supports filtering by date range and employee for payroll processing. Includes hourly rates and pay calculations based on award classifications. Essential for payroll preparation and labor cost analysis.

get.summary: Get all roles available at specific location
get.description: Retrieves all job roles that are available and active at a specific location. Used for employee assignment, scheduling, and role management. Helps managers understand which positions are available at each location and supports workforce planning. Essential for location-based role assignments and scheduling.

post.summary: Add new role to location's available positions
post.description: Associates a job role with a specific location, making it available for employee assignments and scheduling at that location. Validates that the role exists and prevents duplicate assignments. Used for expanding available positions at locations and supporting business growth. Essential for location-based workforce management.

get.summary: Get publicly available location information
get.description: Retrieves basic location information that is publicly accessible without authentication. Used by device registration processes and public-facing interfaces. Returns location names, addresses, and active status. Essential for device setup and location selection during system configuration.

get.summary: Retrieve image file by unique identifier
get.description: Retrieves an image file from cloud storage using its unique identifier. Supports various image formats and automatic format optimization. Used for displaying employee profile photos, clock-in verification images, and other business-related images. Provides secure access to stored images with proper authentication.

get.summary: Get location-specific dashboard statistics
get.description: Retrieves dashboard statistics filtered for a specific location including employee counts, hours worked, and operational metrics. Used by location managers to monitor their specific site performance and make location-based decisions. Essential for multi-location businesses to track individual site performance.

get.summary: Get role-specific dashboard statistics
get.description: Retrieves dashboard statistics filtered for a specific job role including employee counts, hours worked, and performance metrics. Used by managers to monitor role-based performance and identify training needs or staffing gaps. Essential for role-based workforce analysis and management decisions.

post.summary: Register new time clock device for location
post.description: Registers a new time clock device for use at a specific location. Creates device credentials and activation codes for secure device setup. Validates location exists and device information is complete. Used during initial device deployment and replacement. Essential for maintaining secure device access to the time clock system.

get.summary: Get all managed devices and their status
get.description: Retrieves all registered devices with their current status, location assignments, and last activity information. Used by administrators to monitor device health, manage device fleet, and troubleshoot connectivity issues. Essential for maintaining reliable time clock operations across all locations.

post.summary: Activate device using registration code
post.description: Activates a registered device using the activation code provided during registration. Validates the device ID and activation code match. Once activated, the device can be used for employee time clock operations. Used during device setup process to enable time clock functionality.

post.summary: Check device connectivity and operational status
post.description: Checks the current status of a time clock device including connectivity, battery level, and operational state. Used for device monitoring, troubleshooting, and maintenance scheduling. Provides real-time device health information to ensure reliable time clock operations.
